prose: Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene were computed at the GFN2-xTB level (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb backend) via finite difference of Mulliken partial charges on the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) species. Input geometry was the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz; no geometry re-optimization was performed (single-point Fukui evaluation). Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral reference. Partial-charge scheme: Mulliken (GFN2-xTB). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui function), located at Fe (atom 11) = 0.22885456. The maximum f⁻ (nucleophilic Fukui function) is also at Fe = 0.25938191. The dual descriptor at Fe is −0.030527354, indicating net nucleophilic character at iron, consistent with ferrocene's well-known electron-rich metal centre. Both Cp rings are symmetry-equivalent and reproduce perfect five-fold symmetry. Integrity: trustworthy = true; sum(f⁺) = 1.0000; sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No engine warnings were raised.
